voters.comment: "Called to the Devil and the Devil said "Hey." Love. Another fun band name that I've never heard of though I do recognize this song.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I'm not entirely sure when it's supposed to be the devil talking or the singer but best I can tell this is a song about needing someone to talk to you so badly that you reach out to the devil who agrees to help you sort your shit out. The devil lets the singer know that it's fine to take time to yourself to take of yourself and not worry about others. Then, the singer throws it back on the devil when the devil complains about being overworked and too tired. The devil responds by saying that the singer wants everyone to need them or they would let it got or at least accept help. Finally, the singer resolves to just let everything go.

voters.name: Saa

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: I thought maybe about submitting this song as well as Beach Boys California girls just thought it would be interesting to hear the back to back. But felt there were stronger Beach Boys options to submit from Surfin USA, Good Vibrations, Kokomo. Or even next to Carolina Girls.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: This one sounded really familiar to me, so I had to look up if it was based on another song that I might know. Turns out, no, it's an original... but it did lead me to discover that it was created as a West Coast answer to Jay-Z and Alicia Keys' NYC anthem "Empire State of Mind" (which is a pretty good song in its own right).A little too poppy for my taste, but I did enjoy reading up on it since it's a pretty odd collab.

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: So, I found this song on TikTok a while ago and it was used a lot by people who, like me, grew up country, but were generally progressive and it just kind of made me feel seen? Like, it's a really weird dicotomy to grow up with a lot of these experiences and such that aren't bad, but are almost always lumped in with crazy boomers from the same area. Being country or in my case, a domesticated county person, doesn't mean you are a terrible person or that everything you grew up with is trash.

voters.comment: MisterWives requirement met. The playlist is officially in compliance. Good closer.

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: Beautiful. I am a sucker for mournful-hopeful songs, but this particular recording has a sort of tin-y-ness to it I am not a fan of. I found a live recording of the song which doesn't have it and it was great (even considering the potato phone someone was recording on, lol). So, seems to be something to do with their editing for commercial release.

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: A good redemption for MisterWives to me after not feeling the live performance. Now, I'm starting to get concerned that MisterWives may be a weekly feature in our rounds...

best_song.name: Tech Noir 2 (feat. John Carpenter, Charlie Simpson)

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: So excited to see Gunship, and Tech Noir is such a banger! PLUS such a fun video!! Take my points!!

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: So many references to things I love! Zelda, Sonic, Monkey Island... hell they even showed a flash of the exact Dragon Ball cartridge that got me into collecting Super Famicom games! But not only are the visuals cool and very technically impressive, I actually really love the song too. Gunship rules!

voters.name: Dorothy
voters.comment: Great song and I really felt with the video

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: Always a fan of claymation. Great video, music, and references.

voters.name: Amanda
voters.comment: This reminds me of Labyrinth a bit. That poor baby.

voters.comment: So this is the famous cat empire I've heard so much about.

voters.name: [Left the League]
voters.comment: It is my personal policy to not award  points to multiple songs from the same artist and, regretfully I thought The Chariot fit the beach theme slightly better. Definitely looking forward to hearing more from this band though!

voters.name: Sarah
voters.comment: Found myself singing along to this one a couple days ago so it's gotta get a couple votes.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: You know you have at least one vote from me for The Cat Empire! Love the uplifting lyrics on this one. "Lord unbind my feet / Let me mingle with the good people we meet"  🎵P.S. I was listening to this near Owen and he laughed and said, "That guy has a funny voice, right?" lol I guess he kinda does, buddy

I think Hot to Go (I know that's not how it's written, but it's annoying to do correctly) was my favorite until it became the obvious symbol of "This is a low effort, throw away comedy movie" when it showed up in every other movie trailer recently.

voters.comment: I was considering submitting this one so I think I have to vote for it!

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I had a huge discussion with Amanda about Chappell and her ever growing list of controversies. None of them are that bad but it's always her responses that make me feel like she's kind of a selfish asshole. I know that may be surprising that someone at the top of an industry is a selfish, self-centered person, but I'm not afraid to say that I think this may be one of those cases.

FREE BOTH KEVINSAnyway, this was a really catchy song.

voters.comment: If Goldfinger is not in ska week, is it ska week? Also, we've had MisterWives 5 or 6 times at this point. Repeating artists isn't really a problem as long as there's a decent enough gap or just have the big brass balls to repeat. Goldfinger is like pizza. It's never a no. At worst, it's a maybe not right now. Now is fine.

voters.comment: I have great nostalgia for this song. The big bad in one my campaigns was killing the zealot barbarian's patron. I made a special battle music playlist for the climactic battle, so when the character took the mantle of the god of strength and storms this song started playing.

voters.comment: THUNDAH

voters.comment: Color me shocked to see that this did indeed come out 1990...

voters.comment: Man, I really wanted to like this song because of the epic guitar solo at the beginning but I just can't stand the singer's voice. That's always been my problem with AC/DC but I feel like it's especially amplified in this song. He sounds like he's doing a jokey Muppet voice.
